channel.title: Jiǎoluò Shūwū · 角落書屋
channel.description: Notes on Chinese language and civilisation — characters, vocabulary, grammar, history, philosophy, and the world they shaped.

item.title: 白 · white, clear, plain, in vain
item.description: Sunlight on a single point. The character that names the color of mourning, the color of clarity, the empty plate, and the speech that hides nothing.

item.title: 北 · north; northern; to flee
item.description: Two people standing back-to-back — the direction of cold and turning away that became the name of China's capital.

item.title: 东 · east; eastern
item.description: The sun rising behind a tree — and the direction that anchored China's cosmological, political, and poetic imagination.

item.title: 多 · many; much; more; how
item.description: Two evenings stacked — the character that encodes abundance, comparison, and the most common question about quantity in Mandarin.

item.title: 耳 · ear; to hear; handle
item.description: The pictograph of an ear that anchors Chinese words for listening, fame, and the furred handles on bronze vessels.

item.title: 高 · tall; high; elevated; surname
item.description: A tall palace gate rising above the ground — and the character that anchors Chinese words for excellence, happiness, and examination pressure simultaneously.

item.title: 关 · to close, to concern, the frontier pass
item.description: The bolt that closes a gate, the mountain pass that controls a frontier, and the relation that ties two people together. One character holds all three.

item.title: 见 · to see, to meet, to appear
item.description: An eye on legs. The character that built Chinese verbs of seeing, meeting, opinion, and the moment a thing first comes into view.

item.title: 开 · to open, to start, to operate
item.description: Two hands lifting the bar across a closed gate. From that single image flow most of modern life: opening a door, starting a meeting, driving a car, writing a check, blooming.

item.title: 空 · empty, sky, void; in vain
item.description: A cave with the work of hollowing inside it. The character that names the open sky, the empty room, the wasted afternoon, and the Buddhist śūnyatā at the heart of Chan.

item.title: 路 · road, path, route
item.description: The walked road, where 道 is the navigated way: 路 is the surface beneath the foot, the route the messenger takes, the journey of a life measured in distance.

item.title: 目 · eye; to look at; section heading
item.description: The pictograph of an eye that became the Chinese radical for everything seen, watched, and classified.

item.title: 南 · south; southern
item.description: The direction that Chinese buildings face and emperors receive guests — warmth, productivity, and the seat of imperial ritual power.

item.title: 少 · few; little; young
item.description: Small with a stroke removed — the character for scarcity that doubles as the word for youth when read in falling tone.

item.title: 死 · death, to die, dead, fixed
item.description: A figure kneeling beside a corpse. The character behind the strongest verb of finality in Chinese, the bedrock of every taboo, and the intensifier that turns 累 into 累死了.

item.title: 无 · nothing, without, non-being
item.description: A dancer with arms outstretched, holding nothing. The character behind every Chinese negation of having, every Daoist non-doing, and the philosophical pole that pairs with 有 to frame the whole cosmos.

item.title: 西 · west; western
item.description: A bird settling into its nest at sunset — the direction that gave Chinese the word for "things" when it paired with east.

item.title: 新 · new; fresh; recently; novel
item.description: Chopping fresh wood with an axe — the character whose compounds span New Year greetings, fresh produce, marriage, and the great question of what counts as modern.

item.title: 足 · foot; sufficient; enough
item.description: The foot that became the word for sufficiency — and the radical anchoring every character involving legs, running, and roads.

item.title: 端午节 · Dragon Boat Festival
item.description: Rice dumplings thrown into a river for a drowned poet — the festival that fused an act of mourning into one of China's great summer celebrations.

item.title: 集锦 · Collections
item.description: Idioms, curated reading paths, and reference lists. Entries grouped to be read together.

item.title: 探 · Explore
item.description: The master entry point. Three families of entries, every category browsable from one place.

item.title: 语言 · The Language
item.description: Characters, vocabulary, grammar. The building blocks of written and spoken Chinese.

item.title: 话题 · Topics
item.description: Thought, place, time, lived life. What the Chinese language is used to say.

item.title: 呢 · the continuation and reciprocal-question particle
item.description: The particle that turns a finished sentence into an open conversation: softens questions, returns them to the asker, and lets a state hang in the air.

item.title: 传统 · tradition; traditional
item.description: The thread passed down through generations — and the concept that sits at the center of every Chinese debate about modernity, identity, and what to keep.

item.title: 国家 · nation; state; country
item.description: A compound of the two most culturally loaded characters in Chinese — country and family — that encodes the Confucian political imagination in two syllables.

item.title: 文化 · culture; civilization
item.description: The word that names the organizing project of this entire site — and a concept that Chinese thought built from the ground up differently than the West did.

item.title: 中国 · the Middle Kingdom; China
item.description: Two characters older than the country they now name. 中 (center) and 国 (state) joined three thousand years ago to mean "the central states"; the modern country took the phrase for itself only in the 19th century.

item.title: 风 · wind; style; custom
item.description: The character that moves crops, carries seeds, and became the Chinese word for cultural character itself.
